chatbotr 0.1.0

Get started with chatbotr

Source: vignettes/get-started.Rmd
get-started.Rmd

chatbotr provides a floating chat interface for Shiny apps. It is designed to stay out of the way until you need it.

Basic usage

The simplest way to add a chat bubble is using floating_chat_ui() and floating_chat_server(). You must provide an ellmer chat client.

library(shiny)
library(bslib)
library(chatbotr)

ui <- page_fluid(
  floating_chat_ui("chat")
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Requires GITHUB_PAT if using chat_github()
  chat <- ellmer::chat_github()
  floating_chat_server("chat", client = chat)
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

Offcanvas interface

If you prefer a slide-out panel, use the offcanvas_* variants:

ui <- page_fluid(
  offcanvas_chat_ui("chat")
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  floating_chat_server("chat", client = ellmer::chat_github())
}

Let users bring their own key

A common pattern is to let users configure their own LLM client at runtime. chatbotr provides api_settings_ui() and api_settings_server() for this purpose.

ui <- page_fluid(
  api_settings_ui("settings"),
  floating_chat_ui("chat")
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  settings <- api_settings_server("settings")
  
  observe({
    req(settings$is_configured())
    floating_chat_server("chat", client = settings$client())
  })
}

Requirements

Ensure you are using ellmer (>= 0.4.0.9000).

pak::pak("tidyverse/ellmer")

On this page
